core_model.structure_summary: Critical points of an energy functional — a nonlocal quadratic form of fractional order plus a spatially modulated quadratic term minus a spatially modulated nonlinear potential term — are sought on the sphere of fixed squared integral norm, the constraint entering the stationarity equation through an unknown multiplier.
core_model.canonical_form: I(u) = \frac{1}{2}\int |(-\Delta)^{s/2} u|^2\,dx + \frac{1}{2}\int a(\epsilon x)\,u^2\,dx - \int b(\epsilon x)\,G(u)\,dx, \quad \text{critical on } \Big\{u : \int u^2\,dx = c\Big\}; \qquad (-\Delta)^s u + a(\epsilon x)\,u = \mu u + b(\epsilon x)\,g(u) \ \text{in } \mathbb{R}^N

variables.meaning: scalar function on unbounded continuum whose critical-point profile is sought
unknown multiplier enforcing the fixed-norm constraint
fixed value of the squared integral norm
small scaling of the spatial modulation of the coefficient functions
fractional order of the nonlocal operator
energy functional whose constrained critical points are the solutions

deterministic_skeleton.summary: A stationary semilinear nonlocal elliptic equation on an unbounded continuum: a fractional-order smoothing operator plus a spatially modulated linear term balancing a linear multiplier term and a spatially modulated subcritical nonlinearity, subject to a fixed-norm side condition.

notes: The count of solutions is tied to the number of global maximum points of one spatial coefficient in the small-scaling limit. The nonlocal fractional operator is tagged diffusion_term as its closest vocabulary match (Laplacian-type smoothing of noninteger order). The equation is the standing-profile reduction of a first-order-in-time dispersive evolution equation cited as background; the paper works entirely with the stationary constrained problem.
